kept.text:
EXCLUSIVE: @_TalkingTokens sat down with Max Stein, a director on @BlackRock's digital assets team for his first podcast interview.

We get an inside look at BlackRock's digital asset strategy, why tokenized cash will be the next huge opportunity, investor appetite for the industry and more.

TIMESTAMPS
00:00 – Intro
01:18 – How Max defines tokenization and his role at BlackRock
01:40 – How BlackRock goes from idea to product in digital assets
03:16 – Regulatory environment and how it affects the pace of product development
05:21 – Stablecoins at BlackRock: reserve management and internal use
06:17 – Why BlackRock chose @Securitize and how BUIDL was designed
07:54 – How the FTX collapse actually accelerated BlackRock's tokenization work
08:54 – Why tokenized cash is still the biggest opportunity
14:09 – The shift from private chains to public networks and the hybrid model
22:17 – Stablecoins as utility: programmable money, flash loans, and same-rail settlement
27:36 – His thoughts on DeFi exploits, contagion, and what it means for tokenized asset design
33:13 – How AI agents may prefer onchain rails and accelerate crypto adoption
38:50 – Final advice: small decisions shape whether a product scales or sits on a shelf

Full episode below:

kept.text:
.@ethereum is still the undisputed RWAs king with $16.07B in active Mcap.
@binance ​BSC holds $3.8B, followed by @solana $1.4B, and Stellar ($1.2B).

When it comes to the leading platforms, ​

>> @Securitize has $4.26B leading the charge, largely fueled by the BlackRock BUIDL integration.
​>> @tether has $3.33B, dominating the precious metals space with XAUT.
​>> @OndoFinance has $3.01B, it's the gold standard for bringing U.S. Treasury yields to the masses.
>> @circle with $2.90B is successfully scaling the US Yield Coin USYC.
​>> @maplefinance with $2.11B is the powerhouse for institutional private credit.
​>> @Paxos with $2.10B provides the staying power of regulated gold PAXG.
>> ​@centrifuge with $1.32B brings diverse real world credit ecosystems Onchain.
​>> @Spiko_finance has $1.21B, being the rising star for EU T-Bills and Money Markets.
​
When it comes to the yields, ​the composition of these assets tells you exactly what big money wants:

​>> Bonds/Money Market Funds: $15.3B; The absolute lion's share.
​>>Precious Metals: $5.6B.
​>> Private Credit: $2.6B.

In a world of volatile rates, the ability to hold a liquid, Onchain version of a T-Bill or a Money Market Fund is the ultimate utility.

​With 162 major issuers now live and a growth curve that shows no signs of slowing, we're left with how much of the $600T traditional finance market is left to be eaten.

kept.text:
Rob says the Securitize × ComputerShare deal solves one of the problems crypto has not been able to solve on its own:

"Value accrual to tokens has been broken for a long, long time. The equities market has worked for 150 years."

"Now we can take assets that have figured out value accrual and layer on the value propositions of blockchain and permissionless finance."

"ComputerShare manages 60% of the S&P 500. Securitize just signed them as the tokenization partner."

"This is a match made in heaven."

Long $CEPT. NFA.

kept.text:
JUST IN: Computershare Partners with Securitize to Enable Tokenized Shares for U.S.-Listed Companies

Computershare and Securitize have agreed to let U.S.-listed issuers offer Issuer-Sponsored Tokens (ISTs) — blockchain-based shares issued directly alongside traditional equity. These tokens grant holders the same full shareholder rights (voting, dividends, etc.) as conventional shares, with Computershare continuing as the transfer agent of record. The model focuses on direct on-chain issuance rather than derivative wrappers, aiming to improve efficiency while preserving existing regulatory and corporate frameworks.

kept.text:
Most “tokenized stocks” you can buy today aren’t really tokenized stocks. They’re wrappers.

→ xStocks, Ondo, etc, all the same shape: an SPV holds the share, you hold a token against the SPV. You don’t own the stock. You own a claim on a shell that owns the stock. Cap table never sees you. Voting doesn’t pass through.

→ Hyperliquid synthetic stock perps are at least honest about it. Pure price exposure, no rights.

→ That’s why the @Securitize and @Computershare announcement today is big. It‘s the first scaled path to the actual share onchain. 

→ ISTs are the real thing: voting, dividends, corporate actions, recorded on the master securityholder file. And Computershare is transfer agent for 58% of the S&P 500.

That being said, I think wrapped survives in ex-US retail for a while because ISTs launch permissioned by default, but issuers will eventually want that distribution too.

kept.text:
I know you’re bored of seeing this infographics, but I’m going to post it as many times as I can because it explains the current state of tokenized stocks currently - citing the US stocks and global stocks.

The news reaching the tokenization world is that @Securitize has partnered with @Computershare to tokenize US stocks.

Currently, we just have *ONLY $1.2B* in tokenized stocks on functional rails, but what if we look at how much we haven’t tokenized?

> the global public equities has a value of $144T

> the US stocks public equities has a value of $72T

Mathematically, only 0.000833% and 0.001667% of global and US equities respectively have been tokenized.

Securitize who have now partnered with the biggest traditional finance transfer agent itw brings us closer to the goal - and I expect that more stocks in the US and even from other parts of the world especially Hong Kong will come onchain with time.

Tokenization is the biggest narrative right now, and you cannot afford not to be informed.

EVERY NECESSARY ASSET WILL BE TOKENIZED!

Data from @tokenterminal 😎

EXCLUSIVE: @_TalkingTokens sat down with Max Stein, a director on @BlackRock's digital assets team for his first podcast interview.

We get an inside look at BlackRock's digital asset strategy, why tokenized cash will be the next huge opportunity, investor appetite for the industry and more.

TIMESTAMPS
00:00 – Intro
01:18 – How Max defines tokenization and his role at BlackRock
01:40 – How BlackRock goes from idea to product in digital assets
03:16 – Regulatory environment and how it affects the pace of product development
05:21 – Stablecoins at BlackRock: reserve management and internal use
06:17 – Why BlackRock chose @Securitize and how BUIDL was designed
07:54 – How the FTX collapse actually accelerated BlackRock's tokenization work
08:54 – Why tokenized cash is still the biggest opportunity
14:09 – The shift from private chains to public networks and the hybrid model
22:17 – Stablecoins as utility: programmable money, flash loans, and same-rail settlement
27:36 – His thoughts on DeFi exploits, contagion, and what it means for tokenized asset design
33:13 – How AI agents may prefer onchain rails and accelerate crypto adoption
38:50 – Final advice: small decisions shape whether a product scales or sits on a shelf

tweet.text:
$CEPT / @Securitize just cut a deal with Computershare.
For those who don’t know Computershare……. they’re the company that processes your dividends, manages proxy votes, and maintains ownership records for most of corporate America. They went public in 1994 and returned 110x in 7 years. Not because they were flashy. Because they owned the plumbing.

That plumbing is now getting rebuilt on blockchain rails.
And instead of fighting it, Computershare just decided to work with $CEPT / @Securitize to help US listed companies issue their equity in tokenized form.

Think about what that actually means. Computershare has the issuer relationships. $CEPT / @Securitize has the tokenization infrastructure. This agreement hands Securitize a distribution channel into the existing book of U.S. public companies, without having to go knock on every door individually.

The bear case on $CEPT / @Securitize was always “how do they get issuers at scale?”
This is the answer.
NYSE MOU. Currenc tokenization live on Ethereum + Solana. Now Computershare.

Three announcements in five weeks. The rails are being laid in real time.
